title: GitHub - korovin-aa97/talkthrough-mcp: MCP server: turn narrated screen recordings into agent-ready data.
excerpt: MCP server: turn narrated screen recordings into agent-ready data. - korovin-aa97/talkthrough-mcp
content_html:
Quickstart · Tools · FAQ · Troubleshooting · Changelog · Contributing

Feedback ingestion for AI agents. Record your screen and talk; your agent does the rest — files the bugs, writes the spec, builds the backlog.

talkthrough-mcp is a local-first MCP server that turns a narrated screen recording (or any video/audio file) into agent-ready structured data: timestamped transcript segments, optional who-said-what speaker labels, scene-change keyframes, OCR'd on-screen text, and wall-clock anchoring. Everything is served through lazy retrieval tools, so a 30-minute recording never floods the model context — the agent pulls exactly the transcript slice, moment bundle, or frame it needs.

There is no LLM inside the server and no cloud anywhere in the path: ffmpeg, faster-whisper, and RapidOCR run on your machine, and the calling agent brings the intelligence. What makes it different from screen-recorder SaaS and video-analyzer MCPs: it works on arbitrary local files, it ships the agent workflows (server prompts + example agents), and it anchors every timestamp to wall-clock time — so "the moment I said the checkout hung" maps straight to the right window of your server logs.

Quickstart

One command, no system dependencies: ffmpeg falls back to a bundled build, OCR is pip-only, and whisper models download themselves on first use. The only prerequisite is uv (brew install uv or curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh).

Claude Code

Two install paths — pick one, not both (the plugin already includes the server; installing both would register it twice):

Server only — the 7 tools + 5 prompts, and nothing else on your system. Choose this for a minimal setup, or when you manage MCP servers yourself across several clients:

claude mcp add -s user talkthrough -- uvx "talkthrough-mcp[diarization]"

Full plugin — the same server, plus native slash commands (/talkthrough:triage-recording, …) that handle the ceremony for you, a ready-made triage subagent, and an agent skill that teaches Claude the workflow. Choose this for the best out-of-the-box experience:

/plugin marketplace add korovin-aa97/talkthrough-mcp
/plugin install talkthrough@talkthrough

Any other MCP stdio client uses the same server command: uvx "talkthrough-mcp[diarization]". Per-engine folders with exactly these snippets plus verification steps live in integrations/; agents can self-install via llms-install.md.

Who said what (speaker diarization) — included in the configs above

Multi-person recordings (meetings, interviews, panels) can carry S1/S2/… speaker labels. Every install button, snippet, and the plugin above already ship the [diarization] engine, so asking your agent "who said what" just works — diarization itself still runs only when requested per call (process_media(path=..., diarize=true, num_speakers=<count if known>)), and its models download once on first use.

Prefer the minimal server without the diarization engine? Use uvx talkthrough-mcp as the command instead (the MCP registry entry also resolves to this lean form) — an explicit diarize=true will then answer with the one-line install fix. Details in Speakers.

Local checkout (development)

git clone https://github.com/korovin-aa97/talkthrough-mcp
claude mcp add talkthrough -- uv run --directory /path/to/talkthrough-mcp talkthrough-mcp

Then, in your agent:

Process ~/Desktop/recording.mov and triage it — or just invoke the triage-recording server prompt.

Tools

Tool What it does
process_media(path, recorded_at?, vocabulary?, language?, model?, diarize?, num_speakers?, force?) Ingest a video/audio file: local STT, keyframes, OCR, wall-clock, opt-in speaker labels. Returns a compact summary. Idempotent by content hash — re-calls are instant; diarize=true on a processed job adds speakers without re-transcribing.
get_transcript(job_id, start_ms?, end_ms?, format?) Paginated transcript as segments, text, or srt (speaker-prefixed when diarized, plus a roster header); truncation returns next_start_ms.
get_frames(job_id, at_ms? | start_ms?+end_ms?, max_frames?, include_duplicates?) Keyframe images nearest a timestamp or evenly thinned across a range (unique frames by default, max 6/call); each frame names its absolute path.
get_moment(job_id, start_ms, end_ms) The "one remark" bundle: transcript slice + up to 3 frames + their OCR text + wall-clock range (+ speakers_in_range when diarized).
search(job_id, query) Substring search over the transcript AND on-screen OCR text; hits carry t_ms/t_wall, frame refs, and the speaker when diarized.
extract_frame(job_id, at_ms, crop?) Exact-timestamp full-resolution re-extract from the source video (optional crop) when keyframes miss the instant; returns the file's absolute path.
list_jobs() Recent processed recordings with durations, wall-clock starts, counts, and speaker counts when diarized.

Every tool description ships 10+ usage examples, so agents pick the right tool without extra prompting.

Server prompts (slash commands in MCP clients)

Prompt Workflow
triage-recording Narrated screencast → precise findings JSON (bug/feature/question routing, frame evidence)
spec-from-workshop Recorded workshop → structured spec with quoted decisions and open questions
backlog-from-demo Product demo → prioritized backlog with timestamped evidence
meeting-actions Meeting audio → action items, decisions, open questions
correlate-with-logs Recording remarks ↔ system logs via wall-clock windows

The same prompts live as plain files in examples/prompts/ if your client doesn't surface MCP prompts. The findings contract used by triage-recording is examples/output-contract.schema.json.

Works as a skill too (no MCP required)

The same workflow ships as a cross-engine Agent Skill at .agents/skills/talkthrough/ — Claude Code, Codex CLI ($talkthrough), Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, Goose and other SKILL.md-compatible tools read it. Agents without MCP wiring can drive the CLI directly: talkthrough-mcp process recording.mov --json prints the same summary the MCP tool returns, and the job store is shared either way.

Wall-clock anchoring

Every timestamped result carries both t_ms (video-relative) and t_wall (ISO 8601 real time) once the recording start is known. Resolution ladder:

  1. recorded_at parameter (agent/user override) → confidence exact
  2. QuickTime com.apple.quicktime.creationdate tag, carries the local timezone (QuickTime Player recordings; ⌘⇧5 wrote it before macOS 26) → high
  3. Container creation_time tag (UTC) → medium — macOS 26+ ⌘⇧5/ReplayKit screen recordings land here (no creationdate tag anymore); pass recorded_at= when local-tz t_wall matters
  4. File mtime minus duration (recorders finalize files at recording END) → low
  5. Nothing → tools still work with relative t_ms only

Why it matters: "the upload spinner froze here" becomes a ±30 s grep window in your server logs.

Speakers (optional diarization)

With the [diarization] extra installed (included in every generated config — see Quickstart), process_media(diarize=true) labels who said what — locally, like everything else here (sherpa-onnx runtime, no torch, no accounts, no GPU):

  • Speakers become S1, S2, … in order of first appearance; every transcript segment gets a speaker, and the tools surface it everywhere — roster with talk time in get_transcript, speakers_in_range in get_moment, speaker on search hits, S1: prefixes in the text/SRT formats, a speaker count in list_jobs.
  • Know the headcount? Pass num_speakers. Clustering to an exact k removes the main failure mode of unknown-count mode (similar voices merging or one voice splitting). Agents are instructed to do this via the tool guidance; do the same in your own calls.
  • Already processed a recording? Calling process_media(diarize=true) on it re-runs only diarization — whisper is not re-run, and labels appear in the existing job in seconds. Same for changing num_speakers.
  • Labels are anonymous by design; mapping S1 → "Alice" is the calling agent's job (self-introductions, vocatives, the attendees argument of the meeting-actions prompt). The server never guesses names.

Models download once (~47 MB total) from pinned, checksum-verified URLs into ~/.talkthrough/models/; warm runs are zero-network like the rest of the pipeline. Speed on an M-series CPU (4 threads): a 26-minute meeting diarizes in about 2 minutes (RTF ≈ 0.08), on top of the transcription time. Memory: expect on the order of 1–1.5 GB peak RSS while an hour-plus meeting is being diarized (measured on a real 73-minute recording); it is released when the stage completes.

Role Model Download Weights license
Segmentation pyannote segmentation-3.0 (ONNX export by k2-fsa) 7 MB MIT
Embedding (default) NeMo en_titanet_small 40 MB Apache-2.0 (per its NGC model card, the NeMo Toolkit license)
Embedding (alt) WeSpeaker en_voxceleb_resnet34_LM 27 MB CC-BY-4.0
Embedding (alt) 3D-Speaker campplus_sv_en_voxceleb_16k 30 MB Apache-2.0

The default won a real-meeting accept-eval (RU/EN/ES + a 3-speaker 26-minute meeting): it was the only candidate to isolate all three real voices at num_speakers=3, at 2× the speed of the runner-up.

Pick an alternate embedding model (or point at your own .onnx file for offline machines) via TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_EMB_MODEL; tune the unknown-count sensitivity via TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THRESHOLD (see docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md). Honest quality notes live in Limitations.

Privacy

Everything runs locally: your recordings never leave your machine, speech is transcribed by a local whisper model, OCR and speaker diarization are local ONNX inference, and there is no telemetry. The only network access is one-time tool/model downloads (ffmpeg build, whisper model, OCR models, diarization models — the latter pinned by URL + sha256). Diarization keeps no voiceprint database: voice embeddings live only in process memory, and only anonymous turn labels (S1/S2) land on disk.

Languages

Narration in any of Whisper's ~99 languages works: the language is auto-detected per recording, and the summary reports both language and language_probability so agents can tell a confident detection from a shaky one (silence or music at the start can fool the detector — pin it with language="ru" and force=true when that happens). Speaker diarization is acoustic — it fingerprints voices, not words — so it is language-independent and works across all of those languages unchanged.

Pick the model for your languages — per call (model= parameter, agents do this themselves when a transcript comes back garbled) or as the server default (TALKTHROUGH_WHISPER_MODEL):

Model Size Best for
small (default) 464 MB English and major-language narration on CPU
large-v3-turbo ~1.5 GB recommended for non-English — near-large quality at near-small speed
medium ~1.5 GB conservative alternative to turbo
tiny / base 75–145 MB quick drafts, CI
*.en variants — English-only, slightly faster/better for EN

Tips that work in every language: pass product names via vocabulary="Term1, Term2" (biases the decoder so jargon survives), and note that the workflow prompts instruct agents to write digests in the narrator's language while keeping quotes verbatim — the server never translates (exact quotes are evidence; translation is the agent's job).

On-screen text (OCR) defaults to RapidOCR's Latin + Chinese models. For other scripts set TALKTHROUGH_OCR_LANG to your language — ru/uk (→ the eslav pack), ja, ko, ar, hi, el, th, or any RapidOCR pack name like cyrillic — and reprocess with force=true; the matching recognition model downloads once. Spoken-language support is unaffected either way.

Configuration

Env var Default Meaning
TALKTHROUGH_WHISPER_MODEL small default whisper model (tiny/base/small/medium/large-v3/large-v3-turbo); the model tool param overrides per call
TALKTHROUGH_OCR on set off to skip OCR
TALKTHROUGH_OCR_LANG Latin+Chinese recognition script for on-screen text: a language code (ru, ja, ko, ar, hi, …) or a RapidOCR pack name (eslav, cyrillic, latin, …); the model downloads once
TALKTHROUGH_OCR_PARAMS — advanced: JSON object of raw RapidOCR params merged over the derived ones, e.g. {"Rec.lang_type": "cyrillic"}
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZE off set on to diarize by default (needs the [diarization] extra; degrades with a warning without it); an explicit diarize tool param always wins
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THRESHOLD 0.5 clustering sensitivity when num_speakers is unknown: fewer speakers than expected → lower it; more → raise it
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_SEG_MODEL pyannote-segmentation-3-0 segmentation model: allowlist name or a path to a local .onnx (offline preseed)
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_EMB_MODEL nemo_en_titanet_small embedding model: allowlist name (see Speakers) or a local .onnx path
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THREADS min(4, cpus) ONNX threads for both diarization models
TALKTHROUGH_MAX_SECONDS 7200 max media duration
TALKTHROUGH_MAX_FRAMES 600 keyframe budget per job, spread across the whole duration (the 1 s selection floor auto-grows to duration/budget on long recordings)
TALKTHROUGH_HOME ~/.talkthrough job store root

CLI

The pipeline is also a CLI — useful for pre-processing long recordings outside an agent session (the store is content-addressed, so the agent then queries the same job instantly):

talkthrough-mcp process ~/Videos/long-session.mov   # prints the summary
talkthrough-mcp process demo.mov --json             # machine-readable
talkthrough-mcp process sync.m4a --diarize --num-speakers 3   # who said what
talkthrough-mcp gc --keep-days 30                   # clean the job store
talkthrough-mcp serve                               # stdio MCP server (default)

First run notes: missing system ffmpeg triggers a one-time static-ffmpeg download; the first transcription downloads the whisper model (~460 MB for small); both are cached. After that, expect roughly 3× faster than real time on an Apple-Silicon CPU with the default model, OCR included (a 2-minute clip processes in ~40 s) — and instant re-runs on the same file. Progress streams as MCP progress notifications, and the CLI prints stage lines. More: docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md.

Windows

CI runs lint, the unit suite, a full CLI smoke, and a diarize smoke on windows-latest (static-ffmpeg Windows build, whisper tiny transcription, OCR, the instant idempotent re-run, and a speaker-roster assert through the native sherpa-onnx stack). Notes: the per-job lock is POSIX fcntl and degrades to a no-op on Windows — fine for a single-user machine; quote paths with spaces (uv run talkthrough-mcp process "C:\Videos\Screen Recording.mp4"). If something breaks, please open an issue.

Supported inputs

Video: .mov .mp4 .webm .mkv — audio-only: .m4a .mp3 .wav .ogg .flac (transcript tools only; frame tools explain why they're unavailable). Local files only.

Limitations

Honest edges, so you can decide fast:

  • Speaker labels are segment-level and opt-in. Diarization assigns each whisper segment ONE speaker by dominant overlap — a segment spanning a fast exchange gets the majority voice, sub-second interjections ("yeah", "mhm") can be absorbed, and heavy crosstalk degrades clustering (the segmentation model tracks at most 2 simultaneous voices). Quality is pyannote-3.x-generation. The comfort zone without hints is roughly 2–8 speakers; pass num_speakers whenever the headcount is known — it removes the worst failure mode at any size, and it is the way to go for large meetings (10+).
  • Local files only. No URL/YouTube ingestion (#5) — download first.
  • Keyframes + transcript, not motion analysis. A glitch between scene changes can be invisible in the frame set; extract_frame re-checks any instant, but frame-by-frame motion reasoning is your multimodal model's job.
  • STT quality tracks the model you pick. The default small favors speed; non-English narration wants model="large-v3-turbo" (see Languages).
  • OCR reads crisp UI text well; tiny or low-contrast print is best-effort.
  • Wall-clock confidence depends on recorder metadata — worst case pass recorded_at= (see the ladder above).
  • Windows caveats — POSIX lock degrades to a no-op; see the Windows section above.

How it compares

talkthrough cloud recorder SaaS meeting notetakers typical video-analyzer MCPs
Runs fully locally ✅ ❌ ❌ varies
Any local video/audio file ✅ browser/app captures meetings only ✅
Wall-clock anchoring (log correlation) ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌
Who-said-what speaker labels ✅ local, opt-in some ✅ cloud ❌
Ships agent workflows (prompts, skill, findings contract) ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌
OCR of on-screen text, searchable ✅ some ❌ rare

FAQ

Why not just upload the video to a multimodal model (e.g. Gemini)? For a short, non-sensitive clip — do that. The trade-offs appear with length and sensitivity: an hour of screen recording costs on the order of a million tokens per question, the file leaves your machine, and you still can't map a remark to 14:32:07 UTC to grep your server logs. talkthrough indexes once, locally, then answers any number of follow-ups from the index.

Why not screenpipe? Different job. screenpipe is an always-on recorder of your machine going forward (commercial license). It can't open the .mov a teammate or customer just sent you. talkthrough analyzes any file it's handed — the two compose fine.

There are agent skills that "watch" videos. Why a server with an index? Watch-style skills push a budgeted frame dump into the context window (and go sparse on long videos), often call cloud STT for the audio, and keep nothing. talkthrough builds a persistent local index — transcript + OCR, full-text searchable — retrieves exact frames lazily, anchors everything to wall-clock time, and answers the next question without reprocessing.

I use Jam for bug reports — do I need this? Keep Jam for browser bugs: console+network captured at record time is great evidence. talkthrough covers what a browser extension can't — desktop apps, mobile screencasts, ops incidents, meetings, any file — with no account, and correlates with server-side logs via wall-clock time.

Which agent model do I need to drive this? We ran a 150-run battery against v0.2.0 — 6 model configs (Claude haiku/sonnet/opus, Codex gpt-5.5 at two reasoning efforts + gpt-5.4-mini) × 10 verbatim-identical task prompts × 5 real recordings (30 s–73 min, RU/EN, 1–5 speakers), scored by a strict LLM judge plus mechanical evidence checks. Short version: point lookups and search ("who said X and when") worked on every tier tested; minutes-with-owners and evidence-disciplined name mapping want the top tiers; reasoning effort moved results more than model family. Full matrices — score × time × tokens on every intersection — in docs/MODEL-NOTES.md.

Can't I just script ffmpeg + whisper myself? Yes — that's exactly this pipeline. What you'd be rebuilding: scene-change detection with perceptual dedup, OCR, transcript+OCR search, the wall-clock ladder, MCP tools with embedded usage examples, five workflow prompts, and a findings contract. One uvx command instead of an afternoon of glue.

Is it really local? What leaves my machine? Nothing at runtime. The network is used only for one-time downloads (ffmpeg build, whisper/OCR/diarization models). No telemetry. See Privacy — and SECURITY.md treats a violation of this promise as a vulnerability.

For agents & tooling

Machine-readable entry points, so AI agents can install and use this server without a human reading docs:

  • llms-install.md — step-by-step install instructions for agents
  • llms.txt — index of the documentation
  • .agents/skills/talkthrough/SKILL.md — an Agent Skill teaching the tool workflow; discovered automatically inside a checkout by Codex CLI ($talkthrough) and readable by Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI and other SKILL.md-compatible tools
  • AGENTS.md — instructions for coding agents contributing to this repo
  • server.json — MCP registry manifest
  • integrations/ — per-engine adapters, all generated from one source of truth and drift-tested (incl. the Claude Code plugin under integrations/claude-code/)

Roadmap

URL/YouTube ingestion · persistent speaker-name labels (label_speakers) · word-level speaker splitting · cloud STT · embeddings/semantic search · hosted/remote mode · .mcpb bundle · whisper.cpp backend

Local checkout (development) git clone https://github.com/korovin-aa97/talkthrough-mcp claude mcp add talkthrough -- uv run --directory /path/to/talkthrough-mcp talkthrough-mcp Then, in your agent: Process ~/Desktop/recording.mov and triage it — or just invoke the triage-recording server prompt. Tools Tool What it does process_media(path, recorded_at?, vocabulary?, language?, model?, diarize?, num_speakers?, force?) Ingest a video/audio file: local STT, keyframes, OCR, wall-clock, opt-in speaker labels. Returns a compact summary. Idempotent by content hash — re-calls are instant; diarize=true on a processed job adds speakers without re-transcribing. get_transcript(job_id, start_ms?, end_ms?, format?) Paginated transcript as segments, text, or srt (speaker-prefixed when diarized, plus a roster header); truncation returns next_start_ms. get_frames(job_id, at_ms? | start_ms?+end_ms?, max_frames?, include_duplicates?) Keyframe images nearest a timestamp or evenly thinned across a range (unique frames by default, max 6/call); each frame names its absolute path. get_moment(job_id, start_ms, end_ms) The "one remark" bundle: transcript slice + up to 3 frames + their OCR text + wall-clock range (+ speakers_in_range when diarized). search(job_id, query) Substring search over the transcript AND on-screen OCR text; hits carry t_ms/t_wall, frame refs, and the speaker when diarized. extract_frame(job_id, at_ms, crop?) Exact-timestamp full-resolution re-extract from the source video (optional crop) when keyframes miss the instant; returns the file's absolute path. list_jobs() Recent processed recordings with durations, wall-clock starts, counts, and speaker counts when diarized. Every tool description ships 10+ usage examples, so agents pick the right tool without extra prompting. Server prompts (slash commands in MCP clients) Prompt Workflow triage-recording Narrated screencast → precise findings JSON (bug/feature/question routing, frame evidence) spec-from-workshop Recorded workshop → structured spec with quoted decisions and open questions backlog-from-demo Product demo → prioritized backlog with timestamped evidence meeting-actions Meeting audio → action items, decisions, open questions correlate-with-logs Recording remarks ↔ system logs via wall-clock windows The same prompts live as plain files in examples/prompts/ if your client doesn't surface MCP prompts. The findings contract used by triage-recording is examples/output-contract.schema.json. Works as a skill too (no MCP required) The same workflow ships as a cross-engine Agent Skill at .agents/skills/talkthrough/ — Claude Code, Codex CLI ($talkthrough), Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, Goose and other SKILL.md-compatible tools read it. Agents without MCP wiring can drive the CLI directly: talkthrough-mcp process recording.mov --json prints the same summary the MCP tool returns, and the job store is shared either way. Wall-clock anchoring Every timestamped result carries both t_ms (video-relative) and t_wall (ISO 8601 real time) once the recording start is known. Resolution ladder: recorded_at parameter (agent/user override) → confidence exact QuickTime com.apple.quicktime.creationdate tag, carries the local timezone (QuickTime Player recordings; ⌘⇧5 wrote it before macOS 26) → high Container creation_time tag (UTC) → medium — macOS 26+ ⌘⇧5/ReplayKit screen recordings land here (no creationdate tag anymore); pass recorded_at= when local-tz t_wall matters File mtime minus duration (recorders finalize files at recording END) → low Nothing → tools still work with relative t_ms only Why it matters: "the upload spinner froze here" becomes a ±30 s grep window in your server logs. Speakers (optional diarization) With the [diarization] extra installed (included in every generated config — see Quickstart), process_media(diarize=true) labels who said what — locally, like everything else here (sherpa-onnx runtime, no torch, no accounts, no GPU): Speakers become S1, S2, … in order of first appearance; every transcript segment gets a speaker, and the tools surface it everywhere — roster with talk time in get_transcript, speakers_in_range in get_moment, speaker on search hits, S1: prefixes in the text/SRT formats, a speaker count in list_jobs. Know the headcount? Pass num_speakers. Clustering to an exact k removes the main failure mode of unknown-count mode (similar voices merging or one voice splitting). Agents are instructed to do this via the tool guidance; do the same in your own calls. Already processed a recording? Calling process_media(diarize=true) on it re-runs only diarization — whisper is not re-run, and labels appear in the existing job in seconds. Same for changing num_speakers. Labels are anonymous by design; mapping S1 → "Alice" is the calling agent's job (self-introductions, vocatives, the attendees argument of the meeting-actions prompt). The server never guesses names. Models download once (~47 MB total) from pinned, checksum-verified URLs into ~/.talkthrough/models/; warm runs are zero-network like the rest of the pipeline. Speed on an M-series CPU (4 threads): a 26-minute meeting diarizes in about 2 minutes (RTF ≈ 0.08), on top of the transcription time. Memory: expect on the order of 1–1.5 GB peak RSS while an hour-plus meeting is being diarized (measured on a real 73-minute recording); it is released when the stage completes. Role Model Download Weights license Segmentation pyannote segmentation-3.0 (ONNX export by k2-fsa) 7 MB MIT Embedding (default) NeMo en_titanet_small 40 MB Apache-2.0 (per its NGC model card, the NeMo Toolkit license) Embedding (alt) WeSpeaker en_voxceleb_resnet34_LM 27 MB CC-BY-4.0 Embedding (alt) 3D-Speaker campplus_sv_en_voxceleb_16k 30 MB Apache-2.0 The default won a real-meeting accept-eval (RU/EN/ES + a 3-speaker 26-minute meeting): it was the only candidate to isolate all three real voices at num_speakers=3, at 2× the speed of the runner-up. Pick an alternate embedding model (or point at your own .onnx file for offline machines) via TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_EMB_MODEL; tune the unknown-count sensitivity via TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THRESHOLD (see docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md). Honest quality notes live in Limitations. Privacy Everything runs locally: your recordings never leave your machine, speech is transcribed by a local whisper model, OCR and speaker diarization are local ONNX inference, and there is no telemetry. The only network access is one-time tool/model downloads (ffmpeg build, whisper model, OCR models, diarization models — the latter pinned by URL + sha256). Diarization keeps no voiceprint database: voice embeddings live only in process memory, and only anonymous turn labels (S1/S2) land on disk. Languages Narration in any of Whisper's ~99 languages works: the language is auto-detected per recording, and the summary reports both language and language_probability so agents can tell a confident detection from a shaky one (silence or music at the start can fool the detector — pin it with language="ru" and force=true when that happens). Speaker diarization is acoustic — it fingerprints voices, not words — so it is language-independent and works across all of those languages unchanged. Pick the model for your languages — per call (model= parameter, agents do this themselves when a transcript comes back garbled) or as the server default (TALKTHROUGH_WHISPER_MODEL): Model Size Best for small (default) 464 MB English and major-language narration on CPU large-v3-turbo ~1.5 GB recommended for non-English — near-large quality at near-small speed medium ~1.5 GB conservative alternative to turbo tiny / base 75–145 MB quick drafts, CI *.en variants — English-only, slightly faster/better for EN Tips that work in every language: pass product names via vocabulary="Term1, Term2" (biases the decoder so jargon survives), and note that the workflow prompts instruct agents to write digests in the narrator's language while keeping quotes verbatim — the server never translates (exact quotes are evidence; translation is the agent's job). On-screen text (OCR) defaults to RapidOCR's Latin + Chinese models. For other scripts set TALKTHROUGH_OCR_LANG to your language — ru/uk (→ the eslav pack), ja, ko, ar, hi, el, th, or any RapidOCR pack name like cyrillic — and reprocess with force=true; the matching recognition model downloads once. Spoken-language support is unaffected either way. Configuration Env var Default Meaning TALKTHROUGH_WHISPER_MODEL small default whisper model (tiny/base/small/medium/large-v3/large-v3-turbo); the model tool param overrides per call TALKTHROUGH_OCR on set off to skip OCR TALKTHROUGH_OCR_LANG Latin+Chinese recognition script for on-screen text: a language code (ru, ja, ko, ar, hi, …) or a RapidOCR pack name (eslav, cyrillic, latin, …); the model downloads once TALKTHROUGH_OCR_PARAMS — advanced: JSON object of raw RapidOCR params merged over the derived ones, e.g. {"Rec.lang_type": "cyrillic"} TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZE off set on to diarize by default (needs the [diarization] extra; degrades with a warning without it); an explicit diarize tool param always wins TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THRESHOLD 0.5 clustering sensitivity when num_speakers is unknown: fewer speakers than expected → lower it; more → raise it TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_SEG_MODEL pyannote-segmentation-3-0 segmentation model: allowlist name or a path to a local .onnx (offline preseed) TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_EMB_MODEL nemo_en_titanet_small embedding model: allowlist name (see Speakers) or a local .onnx path TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THREADS min(4, cpus) ONNX threads for both diarization models TALKTHROUGH_MAX_SECONDS 7200 max media duration TALKTHROUGH_MAX_FRAMES 600 keyframe budget per job, spread across the whole duration (the 1 s selection floor auto-grows to duration/budget on long recordings) TALKTHROUGH_HOME ~/.talkthrough job store root CLI The pipeline is also a CLI — useful for pre-processing long recordings outside an agent session (the store is content-addressed, so the agent then queries the same job instantly): talkthrough-mcp process ~/Videos/long-session.mov # prints the summary talkthrough-mcp process demo.mov --json # machine-readable talkthrough-mcp process sync.m4a --diarize --num-speakers 3 # who said what talkthrough-mcp gc --keep-days 30 # clean the job store talkthrough-mcp serve # stdio MCP server (default) First run notes: missing system ffmpeg triggers a one-time static-ffmpeg download; the first transcription downloads the whisper model (~460 MB for small); both are cached. After that, expect roughly 3× faster than real time on an Apple-Silicon CPU with the default model, OCR included (a 2-minute clip processes in ~40 s) — and instant re-runs on the same file. Progress streams as MCP progress notifications, and the CLI prints stage lines. More: docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md. Windows CI runs lint, the unit suite, a full CLI smoke, and a diarize smoke on windows-latest (static-ffmpeg Windows build, whisper tiny transcription, OCR, the instant idempotent re-run, and a speaker-roster assert through the native sherpa-onnx stack). Notes: the per-job lock is POSIX fcntl and degrades to a no-op on Windows — fine for a single-user machine; quote paths with spaces (uv run talkthrough-mcp process "C:\Videos\Screen Recording.mp4"). If something breaks, please open an issue. Supported inputs Video: .mov .mp4 .webm .mkv — audio-only: .m4a .mp3 .wav .ogg .flac (transcript tools only; frame tools explain why they're unavailable). Local files only. Limitations Honest edges, so you can decide fast: Speaker labels are segment-level and opt-in. Diarization assigns each whisper segment ONE speaker by dominant overlap — a segment spanning a fast exchange gets the majority voice, sub-second interjections ("yeah", "mhm") can be absorbed, and heavy crosstalk degrades clustering (the segmentation model tracks at most 2 simultaneous voices). Quality is pyannote-3.x-generation. The comfort zone without hints is roughly 2–8 speakers; pass num_speakers whenever the headcount is known — it removes the worst failure mode at any size, and it is the way to go for large meetings (10+). Local files only. No URL/YouTube ingestion (#5) — download first. Keyframes + transcript, not motion analysis. A glitch between scene changes can be invisible in the frame set; extract_frame re-checks any instant, but frame-by-frame motion reasoning is your multimodal model's job. STT quality tracks the model you pick. The default small favors speed; non-English narration wants model="large-v3-turbo" (see Languages). OCR reads crisp UI text well; tiny or low-contrast print is best-effort. Wall-clock confidence depends on recorder metadata — worst case pass recorded_at= (see the ladder above). Windows caveats — POSIX lock degrades to a no-op; see the Windows section above. How it compares talkthrough cloud recorder SaaS meeting notetakers typical video-analyzer MCPs Runs fully locally ✅ ❌ ❌ varies Any local video/audio file ✅ browser/app captures meetings only ✅ Wall-clock anchoring (log correlation) ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌ Who-said-what speaker labels ✅ local, opt-in some ✅ cloud ❌ Ships agent workflows (prompts, skill, findings contract) ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌ OCR of on-screen text, searchable ✅ some ❌ rare FAQ Why not just upload the video to a multimodal model (e.g. Gemini)? For a short, non-sensitive clip — do that. The trade-offs appear with length and sensitivity: an hour of screen recording costs on the order of a million tokens per question, the file leaves your machine, and you still can't map a remark to 14:32:07 UTC to grep your server logs. talkthrough indexes once, locally, then answers any number of follow-ups from the index. Why not screenpipe? Different job. screenpipe is an always-on recorder of your machine going forward (commercial license). It can't open the .mov a teammate or customer just sent you. talkthrough analyzes any file it's handed — the two compose fine. There are agent skills that "watch" videos. Why a server with an index? Watch-style skills push a budgeted frame dump into the context window (and go sparse on long videos), often call cloud STT for the audio, and keep nothing. talkthrough builds a persistent local index — transcript + OCR, full-text searchable — retrieves exact frames lazily, anchors everything to wall-clock time, and answers the next question without reprocessing. I use Jam for bug reports — do I need this? Keep Jam for browser bugs: console+network captured at record time is great evidence. talkthrough covers what a browser extension can't — desktop apps, mobile screencasts, ops incidents, meetings, any file — with no account, and correlates with server-side logs via wall-clock time. Which agent model do I need to drive this? We ran a 150-run battery against v0.2.0 — 6 model configs (Claude haiku/sonnet/opus, Codex gpt-5.5 at two reasoning efforts + gpt-5.4-mini) × 10 verbatim-identical task prompts × 5 real recordings (30 s–73 min, RU/EN, 1–5 speakers), scored by a strict LLM judge plus mechanical evidence checks. Short version: point lookups and search ("who said X and when") worked on every tier tested; minutes-with-owners and evidence-disciplined name mapping want the top tiers; reasoning effort moved results more than model family. Full matrices — score × time × tokens on every intersection — in docs/MODEL-NOTES.md. Can't I just script ffmpeg + whisper myself? Yes — that's exactly this pipeline. What you'd be rebuilding: scene-change detection with perceptual dedup, OCR, transcript+OCR search, the wall-clock ladder, MCP tools with embedded usage examples, five workflow prompts, and a findings contract. One uvx command instead of an afternoon of glue. Is it really local? What leaves my machine? Nothing at runtime. The network is used only for one-time downloads (ffmpeg build, whisper/OCR/diarization models). No telemetry. See Privacy — and SECURITY.md treats a violation of this promise as a vulnerability. For agents & tooling Machine-readable entry points, so AI agents can install and use this server without a human reading docs: llms-install.md — step-by-step install instructions for agents llms.txt — index of the documentation .agents/skills/talkthrough/SKILL.md — an Agent Skill teaching the tool workflow; discovered automatically inside a checkout by Codex CLI ($talkthrough) and readable by Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI and other SKILL.md-compatible tools AGENTS.md — instructions for coding agents contributing to this repo server.json — MCP registry manifest integrations/ — per-engine adapters, all generated from one source of truth and drift-tested (incl. the Claude Code plugin under integrations/claude-code/) Roadmap URL/YouTube ingestion · persistent speaker-name labels (label_speakers) · word-level speaker splitting · cloud STT · embeddings/semantic search · hosted/remote mode · .mcpb bundle · whisper.cpp backend License MIT

blocks.html_en: talkthrough-mcp is a local-first MCP server that turns a narrated screen recording (or any video/audio file) into agent-ready structured data: timestamped transcript segments, optional who-said-what speaker labels, scene-change keyframes, OCR'd on-screen text, and wall-clock anchoring. Everything is served through lazy retrieval tools, so a 30-minute recording never floods the model context — the agent pulls exactly the transcript slice, moment bundle, or frame it needs.

blocks.html_en: There is no LLM inside the server and no cloud anywhere in the path: ffmpeg, faster-whisper, and RapidOCR run on your machine, and the calling agent brings the intelligence. What makes it different from screen-recorder SaaS and video-analyzer MCPs: it works on arbitrary local files, it ships the agent workflows (server prompts + example agents), and it anchors every timestamp to wall-clock time — so "the moment I said the checkout hung" maps straight to the right window of your server logs.

blocks.html_en: • Speakers become S1, S2, … in order of first appearance; every transcript segment gets a speaker, and the tools surface it everywhere — roster with talk time in get_transcript, speakers_in_range in get_moment, speaker on search hits, S1: prefixes in the text/SRT formats, a speaker count in list_jobs.

blocks.html_en: Models download once (~47 MB total) from pinned, checksum-verified URLs into ~/.talkthrough/models/; warm runs are zero-network like the rest of the pipeline. Speed on an M-series CPU (4 threads): a 26-minute meeting diarizes in about 2 minutes (RTF ≈ 0.08), on top of the transcription time. Memory: expect on the order of 1–1.5 GB peak RSS while an hour-plus meeting is being diarized (measured on a real 73-minute recording); it is released when the stage completes.

隐私

blocks.html_en: Everything runs locally: your recordings never leave your machine, speech is transcribed by a local whisper model, OCR and speaker diarization are local ONNX inference, and there is no telemetry. The only network access is one-time tool/model downloads (ffmpeg build, whisper model, OCR models, diarization models — the latter pinned by URL + sha256). Diarization keeps no voiceprint database: voice embeddings live only in process memory, and only anonymous turn labels (S1/S2) land on disk.

sentences.zh: 一切都在本地运行：您的录音永远不会离开您的机器，语音由本地耳语模型转录，OCR 和说话者分类是本地 ONNX 推理，并且没有遥测。

sentences.en: The only network access is one-time tool/model downloads (ffmpeg build, whisper model, OCR models, diarization models — the latter pinned by URL + sha256).
sentences.zh: 唯一的网络访问是一次性工具/模型下载（ffmpeg 构建、耳语模型、OCR 模型、二值化模型 - 后者由 URL + sha256 固定）。

sentences.en: Diarization keeps no voiceprint database: voice embeddings live only in process memory, and only anonymous turn labels (S1/S2) land on disk.
sentences.zh: Diarization 不保留声纹数据库：语音嵌入仅存在于进程内存中，并且仅匿名转向标签 (S1/S2) 位于磁盘上。
语言

blocks.html_en: Narration in any of Whisper's ~99 languages works: the language is auto-detected per recording, and the summary reports both language and language_probability so agents can tell a confident detection from a shaky one (silence or music at the start can fool the detector — pin it with language="ru" and force=true when that happens). Speaker diarization is acoustic — it fingerprints voices, not words — so it is language-independent and works across all of those languages unchanged.

sentences.en: Narration in any of Whisper's ~99 languages works: the language is auto-detected per recording, and the summary reports both language and language_probability so agents can tell a confident detection from a shaky one (silence or music at the start can fool the detector — pin it with language="ru" and force=true when that happens).
sentences.zh: Whisper 的约 99 种语言中的任何一种都可以进行旁白：每次录音都会自动检测语言，摘要会报告语言和语言概率，以便代理可以区分可靠的检测和不稳定的检测（开始时的沉默或音乐可以欺骗检测器 - 当发生这种情况时，将其固定为 language="ru" 并强制=true）。

sentences.en: Speaker diarization is acoustic — it fingerprints voices, not words — so it is language-independent and works across all of those languages unchanged.
sentences.zh: 说话人分类是声学的——它对声音而不是单词进行指纹识别——因此它与语言无关，并且在所有这些语言中都可以保持不变。

blocks.html_en: Tips that work in every language: pass product names via vocabulary="Term1, Term2" (biases the decoder so jargon survives), and note that the workflow prompts instruct agents to write digests in the narrator's language while keeping quotes verbatim — the server never translates (exact quotes are evidence; translation is the agent's job).

blocks.html_en: On-screen text (OCR) defaults to RapidOCR's Latin + Chinese models. For other scripts set TALKTHROUGH_OCR_LANG to your language — ru/uk (→ the eslav pack), ja, ko, ar, hi, el, th, or any RapidOCR pack name like cyrillic — and reprocess with force=true; the matching recognition model downloads once. Spoken-language support is unaffected either way.

blocks.html_en: First run notes: missing system ffmpeg triggers a one-time static-ffmpeg download; the first transcription downloads the whisper model (~460 MB for small); both are cached. After that, expect roughly 3× faster than real time on an Apple-Silicon CPU with the default model, OCR included (a 2-minute clip processes in ~40 s) — and instant re-runs on the same file. Progress streams as MCP progress notifications, and the CLI prints stage lines. More: docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md.

blocks.html_en: • Speaker labels are segment-level and opt-in. Diarization assigns each whisper segment ONE speaker by dominant overlap — a segment spanning a fast exchange gets the majority voice, sub-second interjections ("yeah", "mhm") can be absorbed, and heavy crosstalk degrades clustering (the segmentation model tracks at most 2 simultaneous voices). Quality is pyannote-3.x-generation. The comfort zone without hints is roughly 2–8 speakers; pass num_speakers whenever the headcount is known — it removes the worst failure mode at any size, and it is the way to go for large meetings (10+).

质量为 pyannote-3.x 代。

blocks.html_en: Why not just upload the video to a multimodal model (e.g. Gemini)? For a short, non-sensitive clip — do that. The trade-offs appear with length and sensitivity: an hour of screen recording costs on the order of a million tokens per question, the file leaves your machine, and you still can't map a remark to 14:32:07 UTC to grep your server logs. talkthrough indexes once, locally, then answers any number of follow-ups from the index.

blocks.html_en: There are agent skills that "watch" videos. Why a server with an index? Watch-style skills push a budgeted frame dump into the context window (and go sparse on long videos), often call cloud STT for the audio, and keep nothing. talkthrough builds a persistent local index — transcript + OCR, full-text searchable — retrieves exact frames lazily, anchors everything to wall-clock time, and answers the next question without reprocessing.

blocks.html_en: Which agent model do I need to drive this? We ran a 150-run battery against v0.2.0 — 6 model configs (Claude haiku/sonnet/opus, Codex gpt-5.5 at two reasoning efforts + gpt-5.4-mini) × 10 verbatim-identical task prompts × 5 real recordings (30 s–73 min, RU/EN, 1–5 speakers), scored by a strict LLM judge plus mechanical evidence checks. Short version: point lookups and search ("who said X and when") worked on every tier tested; minutes-with-owners and evidence-disciplined name mapping want the top tiers; reasoning effort moved results more than model family. Full matrices — score × time × tokens on every intersection — in docs/MODEL-NOTES.md.

content_html_zh:
快速入门 · 工具 · 常见问题解答 · 故障排除 · 变更日志 · 贡献

AI 代理的反馈摄取。 录制您的屏幕并讲话；您的代理会完成剩下的工作 - 提交错误、编写规范、构建积压工作。

talkthrough-mcp 是本地优先的 MCP 服务器，可将叙述的屏幕录制（或任何视频/音频文件）转换为代理就绪的结构化数据：带时间戳的转录片段、可选的谁说了什么说话者标签、场景变化关键帧、OCR 屏幕文本和挂钟锚定。 一切都是通过惰性检索工具提供的，因此 30 分钟的记录永远不会淹没模型上下文 - 代理准确地提取它需要的转录片段、时刻束或框架。

服务器内部没有 LLM，路径中的任何地方都没有云：ffmpeg、faster-whisper 和 RapidOCR 在您的计算机上运行，​​调用代理带来智能。 它与屏幕录像机 SaaS 和视频分析器 MCP 的不同之处在于：它适用于任意本地文件，它提供代理工作流程（服务器提示 + 示例代理），并将每个时间戳锚定到挂钟时间 - 因此“我说结帐挂起的那一刻”直接映射到服务器日志的右侧窗口。

快速入门

一个命令，没有系统依赖性：ffmpeg 回退到捆绑构建，OCR 仅限 pip，而耳语模型在第一次使用时会自行下载。 唯一的先决条件是 uv（brew install uv 或curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh）。

克劳德·科德

两个安装路径 - 选择一个，而不是两者（插件已包含服务器；安装两者将注册两次）：

仅服务器 — 7 个工具 + 5 个提示，系统上没有其他任何东西。 选择此选项进行最小设置，或者当您自己跨多个客户端管理 MCP 服务器时：

claude mcp add -s user talkthrough -- uvx "talkthrough-mcp[diarization]"

完整插件 - 同一服务器，加上本机斜线命令（/talkthrough：triage-recording，...） 为您处理仪式、现成的分诊分代理以及教克劳德工作流程的代理技能。 选择此项以获得最佳的开箱即用体验：

/plugin marketplace add korovin-aa97/talkthrough-mcp
/plugin install talkthrough@talkthrough

谁说了什么（说话者分类）——包含在上面的配置中

多人录音（会议、采访、小组讨论）可携带 S1/S2/... 扬声器标签。 上面的每个安装按钮、代码片段和插件都已经附带了 [diarization] 引擎，因此询问您的代理“谁说了什么”就可以了 — diarization 本身仍然仅在每次调用请求时才运行 (process_media(path=..., diarize=true, num_speakers=<count ifknown>))，并且其模型在第一次使用时下载一次。

更喜欢没有分类引擎的最小服务器？ 使用 uvx talkthrough-mcp 作为命令（MCP 注册表项也解析为这种精简形式）——显式的 diarize=true 将通过单行安装修复进行应答。 详情见演讲者。

本地结账（开发）

git clone https://github.com/korovin-aa97/talkthrough-mcp
claude mcp add talkthrough -- uv run --directory /path/to/talkthrough-mcp talkthrough-mcp

然后，在你的代理中：

处理 ~/Desktop/recording.mov 并对其进行分类 - 或者只是调用分类记录服务器提示符。

Tools

Tool 它的作用
process_media（路径、recorded_at？、词汇？、语言？、模型？、diarize？、num_speakers？、force？） 摄取视频/音频文件：本地 STT、关键帧、OCR、挂钟、选择加入扬声器标签。 返回一个紧凑的摘要。 通过内容哈希实现幂等——重新调用是即时的；已处理作业上的 diarize=true 会添加发言者而无需重新转录。
get_transcript(job_id, start_ms?, end_ms?, 格式?) 以段、文本或 srt 形式分页的转录本（日记化时添加说话者前缀，加上名册标题）；截断返回next_start_ms。
get_frames(job_id, at_ms? | start_ms?+end_ms?, max_frames?, include_duplicates?) 最接近时间戳或在一定范围内均匀细化的关键帧图像（默认情况下为唯一帧，每次调用最多 6 个）；每个帧都会命名其绝对路径。
get_moment（作业 ID、开始时间、结束时间） “一个评论”捆绑包：转录切片 + 最多 3 帧 + 其 OCR 文本 + 挂钟范围（+ 分类时的发言者范围）。
搜索（职位 ID，查询） 对文字记录和屏幕 OCR 文本进行子字符串搜索；当分类时，命中携带 t_ms/t_wall、帧参考和扬声器。
extract_frame(job_id、at_ms、作物？) 当关键帧错过瞬间时，从源视频中重新提取精确时间戳全分辨率（可选裁剪）；返回文件的绝对路径。
职位列表() 最近处理的录音，包括持续时间、挂钟启动、计数以及日记时的发言者计数。

每个工具描述都附带 10 多个使用示例，因此客服人员无需额外提示即可选择正确的工具。

也可作为一项技能（无需 MCP）

挂钟锚定

一旦知道记录开始，每个带时间戳的结果都会包含 t_ms（视频相对）和 t_wall（ISO 8601 实时）。 分辨率阶梯：

  1. Recorded_at 参数（代理/用户覆盖）→ 准确置信度
  2. QuickTime com.apple.quicktime.creationdate 标签，携带本地时区（QuickTime Player 录音；⌘⇧5 在 macOS 26 之前编写）→ 高
  3. 容器创建时间标记 (UTC) → 中 — macOS 26+ ⌘⇧5/ReplayKit 屏幕录制位于此处（不再有创建日期标记）；当 local-tz t_wall 很重要时，传递 Recorded_at=
  4. 文件 mtime 减去持续时间（录音机在录音结束时完成文件）→ 低
  5. 什么都没有 → 工具仍然仅适用于相对 t_ms

为什么重要：“上传旋转器冻结在这里”变成服务器日志中±30秒的grep窗口。

扬声器（可选二值化）

安装了额外的 [diarization]（包含在每个生成的配置中 — 请参阅快速入门），process_media(diarize=true) 会在本地标记谁说了什么，就像这里的其他内容一样（sherpa-onnx 运行时、没有 torch、没有帐户、没有 GPU）：

  • 扬声器变为 S1、S2…… 按首次出现的顺序；每个转录片段都有一个发言者，工具将其呈现在各处 - get_transcript 中包含通话时间的花名册，get_moment 中的发言者_in_range，搜索命中的发言者，S1：文本/SRT 格式中的前缀，list_jobs 中的发言者计数。
  • 知道人数吗？ 传递 num_speakers。 聚类到精确的 k 消除了未知计数模式的主要故障模式（相似的语音合并或一个语音分裂）。 通过工具指南指导客服人员执行此操作；在您自己的通话中执行相同的操作。
  • 已经处理了录音？ 在其上调用 process_media(diarize=true) 只会重新运行二值化 - 不会重新运行耳语，并且标签会在几秒钟内出现在现有作业中。 更改 num_speakers 也是如此。
  • 标签在设计上是匿名的；映射 S1 →“Alice”是呼叫代理的工作（自我介绍、呼唤、会议操作提示的与会者论点）。 服务器永远不会猜测名字。

模型从固定的、经过校验和验证的 URL 下载一次（总共约 47 MB​​）到 ~/.talkthrough/models/；与管道的其余部分一样，热运行也是零网络的。 M 系列 CPU（4 线程）上的速度：26 分钟的会议在大约 2 分钟内进行记录（RTF ≈ 0.08），加上转录时间。 内存：当记录一个多小时的会议时，预计 RSS 峰值约为 1–1.5 GB（根据真实的 73 分钟录音测量）；当阶段完成时它被释放。

Role Model 下载 举重许可证
分割 pyannoteegmentation-3.0（k2-fsa 导出的 ONNX） 7 MB MIT
嵌入（默认） NeMo en_titanet_small 40 MB Apache-2.0（根据其 NGC 模型卡、NeMo Toolkit 许可证）
嵌入（替代） WeSpeaker en_voxceleb_resnet34_LM 27 MB CC-BY-4.0
嵌入（替代） 3D扬声器campplus_sv_en_voxceleb_16k 30 MB 阿帕奇-2.0

默认赢得了真实会议接受评估（RU/EN/ES + 3 个发言者 26 分钟的会议）：它是唯一能够在 num_speakers=3 时隔离所有三个真实声音的候选者，速度是亚军的 2 倍。

通过 TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_EMB_MODEL 选择备用嵌入模型（或针对离线计算机指向您自己的 .onnx 文件）；通过 TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THRESHOLD 调整未知计数灵敏度（请参阅 docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md）。 诚实的品质笔记存在于局限性​​中。

隐私

一切都在本地运行：您的录音永远不会离开您的机器，语音由本地耳语模型转录，OCR 和说话者分类是本地 ONNX 推理，并且没有遥测。 唯一的网络访问是一次性工具/模型下载（ffmpeg 构建、耳语模型、OCR 模型、二值化模型 - 后者由 URL + sha256 固定）。 Diarization 不保留声纹数据库：语音嵌入仅存在于进程内存中，并且仅匿名转向标签 (S1/S2) 位于磁盘上。

语言

Whisper 的约 99 种语言中的任何一种都可以进行旁白：每次录音都会自动检测语言，摘要会报告语言和语言概率，以便代理可以区分可靠的检测和不稳定的检测（开始时的沉默或音乐可以欺骗检测器 - 当发生这种情况时，将其固定为 language="ru" 并强制=true）。 说话人分类是声学的——它对声音而不是单词进行指纹识别——因此它与语言无关，并且在所有这些语言中都可以保持不变。

选择适合您的语言的模型 - 每次调用（模型=参数，当转录结果出现乱码时，代理会自行执行此操作）或作为服务器默认值（TALKTHROUGH_WHISPER_MODEL）：

Model Size 最适合
小（默认） 464 MB CPU 上的英语和主要语言旁白
大型 V3 涡轮增压 ~1.5 GB 推荐用于非英语语言 — 近乎大的质量，近乎小的速度
中等的 ~1.5 GB 涡轮增压的保守替代方案
微小/底座 75–145 MB 快速草稿，CI
*.变体 — 仅限英语，EN 稍快/更好

适用于每种语言的提示：通过词汇表=“Term1，Term2”传递产品名称（使解码器有偏差，以便行话得以保留），并注意工作流程提示指示代理以叙述者的语言编写摘要，同时逐字保留引号 - 服务器永远不会翻译（确切的引用是证据；翻译是代理的工作）。

屏幕文本 (OCR) 默认为 RapidOCR 的拉丁文 + 中文模型。 对于其他脚本，将 TALKTHROUGH_OCR_LANG 设置为您的语言 — ru/uk（→ eslav 包）、ja、ko、ar、hi、el、th 或任何 RapidOCR 包名称（如西里尔语） — 并使用 force=true 重新处理；匹配的识别模型下载一次。 无论哪种方式，口语支持都不受影响。

配置

环境是 默认 意义
TALKTHROUGH_WHISPER_MODEL small 默认耳语模型（tiny/base/small/medium/large-v3/large-v3-turbo）；每次调用都会覆盖模型工具参数
TALKTHROUGH_OCR on 开始跳过 OCR
TALKTHROUGH_OCR_LANG 拉丁语+中文 屏幕文本的识别脚本：语言代码（ru、ja、ko、ar、hi...） 或 RapidOCR 包名称（eslav、西里尔字母、拉丁字母……）；模型下载一次
TALKTHROUGH_OCR_PARAMS — 高级：原始 RapidOCR 参数的 JSON 对象合并到派生参数上，例如{"Rec.lang_type": "西里尔文"}
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZE off 默认情况下设置为 diarize（需要额外的 [diarization]；如果没有它，则会降级并发出警告）；显式的 diarize 工具参数总是获胜
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THRESHOLD 0.5 当 num_speakers 未知时的聚类灵敏度：说话者数量少于预期 → 降低；更多 → 提高它
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_SEG_MODEL pyannote-segmentation-3-0 分段模型：白名单名称或本地 .onnx 路径（离线预置）
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_EMB_MODEL nemo_en_titanet_small 嵌入模型：白名单名称（请参阅发言人）或本地 .onnx 路径
TALKTHROUGH_DIARIZATION_THREADS 分钟（4，CPU） 适用于两种二值化模型的 ONNX 线程
TALKTHROUGH_MAX_SECONDS 7200 最大媒体持续时间
TALKTHROUGH_MAX_FRAMES 600 每个作业的关键帧预算，分布在整个持续时间（1 秒选择层自动增长到长录音的持续时间/预算）
TALKTHROUGH_HOME ~/.talkthrough 作业存储根

CLI

该管道也是一个 CLI - 对于在代理会话之外预处理长记录非常有用（存储是内容寻址的，因此代理会立即查询相同的作业）：

talkthrough-mcp process ~/Videos/long-session.mov   # prints the summary
talkthrough-mcp process demo.mov --json             # machine-readable
talkthrough-mcp process sync.m4a --diarize --num-speakers 3   # who said what
talkthrough-mcp gc --keep-days 30                   # clean the job store
talkthrough-mcp serve                               # stdio MCP server (default)

首次运行注意事项：缺少系统 ffmpeg 触发一次性 static-ffmpeg 下载；第一个转录下载耳语模型（小型约 460 MB）；两者都被缓存。 之后，在默认型号的 Apple-Silicon CPU 上，预计比实时速度快大约 3 倍，包括 OCR（2 分钟的剪辑处理时间约为 40 秒），并且可以在同一文件上即时重新运行。 进度流作为 MCP 进度通知，CLI 打印阶段线。 更多信息：docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md。

视窗

CI 在最新的 Windows 上运行 lint、单元套件、完整的 CLI 烟雾和 diarize 烟雾（静态 ffmpeg Windows 构建、耳语微小转录、OCR、即时幂等重新运行以及通过本机 sherpa-onnx 堆栈进行发言者名册断言）。 注意：每个作业的锁定是 POSIX fcntl，在 Windows 上降级为无操作 — 对于单用户计算机来说很好；用空格引用路径（uv run talkthrough-mcp process "C:\Videos\Screen Recording.mp4"）。 如果出现问题，请提出问题。

支持的输入

视频：.mov .mp4 .webm .mkv — 仅音频：.m4a .mp3 .wav .ogg .flac（仅限转录工具；框架工具解释了它们不可用的原因）。 仅本地文件。

局限性

诚实的优势，让您可以快速做出决定：

  • 演讲者标签是分段级别的且可选择加入。 二分化通过显着重叠为每个耳语片段分配一个说话者——跨越快速交换的片段获得大多数声音，亚秒感叹词（“是的”，“嗯”）可以被吸收，而严重的串扰会降低聚类效果（分段模型最多跟踪 2 个同时的声音）。 质量为 pyannote-3.x 代。 没有提示的舒适区大约是 2-8 个发言者；只要人数已知，就传递 num_speakers — 它消除了任何规模的最糟糕的故障模式，并且是大型会议（10 人以上）的最佳选择。
  • 仅本地文件。 无 URL/YouTube 提取 (#5) — 首先下载。
  • 关键帧+转录本，而不是运动分析。 场景变化之间的故障在帧集中是不可见的； extract_frame 会重新检查任何瞬间，但逐帧运动推理是多模态模型的工作。
  • STT 质量跟踪您选择的型号。 默认的小值有利于速度；非英语旁白需要 model="large-v3-turbo" （请参阅语言）。
  • OCR 可以很好地读取清晰的 UI 文本；尽量使用小或低对比度的打印。
  • 挂钟置信度取决于记录器元数据——最坏情况通过recorded_at=（参见上面的梯子）。
  • Windows 警告 — POSIX 锁降级为无操作；请参阅上面的 Windows 部分。

FAQ

为什么不直接将视频上传到多模式模型（例如 Gemini）？ 对于一个简短的、不敏感的剪辑——就这样做。 权衡出现在长度和灵敏度上：每个问题一小时的屏幕录制成本约为一百万个令牌，文件离开您的计算机，并且您仍然无法将注释映射到 14:32:07 UTC 来 grep 您的服务器日志。 talkthrough 在本地索引一次，然后从索引中回答任意数量的后续内容。

为什么不用屏风管？ 不同的工作。 screenpipe 是一个永远在线的机器记录器（商业许可证）。 它无法打开队友或客户刚刚发送给您的 .mov。 talkthrough 会分析它收到的任何文件——两者组合得很好。

有“观看”视频的特工技能。 为什么服务器有索引？ 观看式技能将预算帧转储推入上下文窗口（并且在长视频上变得稀疏），通常调用云 STT 来获取音频，并且不保留任何内容。 talkthrough 构建持久的本地索引 - 转录 + OCR，可全文搜索 - 延迟检索精确的帧，将所有内容锚定到挂钟时间，并回答下一个问题而无需重新处理。

我使用 Jam 来报告错误——我需要这个吗？ 保留浏览器错误的 Jam：在记录时间捕获的控制台+网络是很好的证据。 talkthrough 涵盖了浏览器扩展无法实现的功能——桌面应用程序、移动屏幕录像、操作事件、会议、任何文件——无需帐户，并通过挂钟时间与服务器端日志相关联。

我需要哪种代理模型来驱动它？ 我们在 v0.2.0 上运行了 150 次电池 — 6 个模型配置（Claude haiku/sonnet/opus、Codex gpt-5.5，两次推理努力 + gpt-5.4-mini）× 10 个逐字相同的任务提示 × 5 个真实录音（30 秒–73 分钟，RU/EN，1–5 个发言者），由严格的 LLM 法官加上机械证据检查进行评分。 简短版本：点查找和搜索（“谁说 X 以及何时”）适用于测试的每个层；与所有者的分钟交流和严格的名称映射需要顶级；推理努力比模型家庭更能推动结果。 完整矩阵 — 每个交叉点上的分数 × 时间 × 标记 — 在 docs/MODEL-NOTES.md 中。

我不能自己编写 ffmpeg + 耳语脚本吗？ 是的——这正是这条管道。 您要重建的内容：带有感知重复数据删除的场景变化检测、OCR、转录+OCR 搜索、挂钟梯子、带有嵌入式使用示例的 MCP 工具、五个工作流程提示和调查结果合同。 一个 uvx 命令，而不是一下午的胶水。

真的是本地的吗？ 我的机器还剩下什么？ 运行时什么也没有。 该网络仅用于一次性下载（ffmpeg 构建、耳语/OCR/二值化模型）。 没有遥测。 请参阅隐私 - SECURITY.md 将违反此承诺视为漏洞。

对于代理和工具

机器可读的入口点，因此人工智能代理可以安装和使用该服务器，而无需人工阅读文档：

  • llms-install.md — 代理的分步安装说明
  • llms.txt — 文档索引
  • .agents/skills/talkthrough/SKILL.md — 教授工具工作流程的代理技能；由 Codex CLI ($talkthrough) 在结帐内自动发现，并可由 Claude Code、Cursor、Copilot、Gemini CLI 和其他 SKILL.md 兼容工具读取
  • AGENTS.md — 为该存储库做出贡献的编码代理的说明
  • server.json — MCP 注册表清单
  • Integrations/ — 每个引擎的适配器，全部由一个事实来源生成并经过漂移测试（包括。 Integrations/claude-code/ 下的 Claude Code 插件）
